stimulus: Overbrowsing by white-tailed deer has caused many eastern North American hardwood forests to be replaced by browse-line glades—forest understories stripped of saplings and covered in invasive shrubs that deer avoid eating. Invasive shrubs in browse-line glades persist in a state of low palatability that lessens their food value—and thus their appeal—to many large herbivores. Maria Vianello and colleagues penned American bison, a once-abundant herbivore species that has been reintroduced to several eastern preserves only in recent years, in paddocks that each contained a palatable shrub and an unpalatable shrub. The researchers found that the bison selected the palatable shrub in 44.1% of trials and the unpalatable shrub in 39.8% of trials, suggesting that ______blank
stem: Which choice most logically completes the text?

answerOptions.content: American bison are reluctant to feed on both palatable and unpalatable shrubs and are therefore unlikely to thrive in eastern hardwood forests.
American bison are less likely to consume invasive shrubs in browse-line glades than other species of large herbivores are, putting bison at a high risk of extinction.
American bison are willing to browse shrubs, but if given a choice, they will eat other more nutritious plants instead.
American bison do not always avoid foraging on unpalatable shrubs, making American bison population recovery a potentially important tool for controlling browse-line glades.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The text explains that browse-line glades are dominated by unpalatable invasive shrubs that most large herbivores avoid, but that in the experiment, reintroduced American bison consumed the unpalatable shrubs in nearly 40% of trials, even when palatable shrubs were available. Because the bison did not always avoid the unpalatable shrubs, growing the bison population could help control browse-line glades by removing those shrubs.

Choice A is incorrect because the bison did eat both kinds of shrubs in significant fractions of trials, not refused them. Choice B is incorrect because the text doesn't compare bison consumption rates to those of other large herbivores. Choice C is incorrect because the text doesn't mention other, more nutritious plants or bison preferences for them.
